meta.note: Reciprocity means: a resident of state A working in state B pays income tax only to state A. The employee files an exemption certificate with their employer to stop state B withholding. Agreements are bilateral between the listed pairs. DC has an additional unilateral policy of not taxing any nonresident employees — represented by dc_nonresident_exemption below.

dc_nonresident_exemption.description: DC does not tax nonresident employees regardless of origin state. A nonresident working in DC files state tax only with their home state. Stacks on top of the bilateral MD-DC and VA-DC agreements above.

no_income_tax_states.name: New Hampshire
no_income_tax_states.note: Previously taxed interest and dividends only; fully phased out after TY2024.

capital_gains_only_states.name: Washington
capital_gains_only_states.note: No broad income tax, but Washington taxes long-term capital gains above a threshold (created 2022, upheld 2023). Partial-income-tax state.

notable_non_reciprocity_borders.note: No reciprocity. Cross-border commuters file nonresident in work state and claim resident credit in home state.
CA has no reciprocity with any state.
MA has no reciprocity with any state.
